summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reading 2 Corinthians 12:7-10, focusing on Paul's 'thorn in the flesh.' He acknowledged that everyone can relate to a personal 'thorn'—whether it's emotional trauma, guilt, physical sickness, or relational pain—that irritates, hurts, and seems persistent. The central message of the sermon was that 'there is no pain in vain.' Pastor Travis taught that God allows pain for a divine purpose and wants to use our deepest hurts for good. He outlined five key purposes for pain: it keeps us humble, keeps us dependent on God, makes us stronger, refines our character, and prepares us for our God-given purpose. Using personal testimony from his own recovery from a brain aneurysm and stroke, he illustrated how God refines us through fiery trials. He emphasized that our weaknesses are where Christ's power rests most visibly and that our brokenness can become a trophy of God's grace. The call was to not waste our pain but to allow God to use it to comfort others, turning our misery into ministry. The sermon concluded with an invitation to accept Jesus Christ and experience His healing and purpose.
mainPoints: Pain keeps us humble and dependent on God, preventing pride and self-reliance.
Pain refines our character and makes us stronger, as God's power is perfected in our weakness.
Pain prepares us for our purpose, equipping us to minister comfort to others from our own experiences.

versesMentioned.text: Therefore, in order to keep me from becoming conceited, I was given a thorn in my flesh, a messenger of Satan, to torment me. Three times I pleaded with the Lord to take it away from me. But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
God did extraordinary miracles through Paul, so that even handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched him were taken to the sick, and their illnesses were cured and the evil spirits left them.

Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me; yet not my will, but yours be done.' An angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him. And being in anguish, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground.
Indeed, we felt we had received the sentence of death. But this happened that we might not rely on ourselves but on God, who raises the dead.

So I went down to the potter’s house, and I saw him working at the wheel. But the pot he was shaping from the clay was marred in his hands; so the potter formed it into another pot, shaping it as seemed best to him. Then the word of the Lord came to me. He said, 'Can I not do with you, Israel, as this potter does?' declares the Lord. 'Like clay in the hand of the potter, so are you in my hand, Israel.'

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.
See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.
Sometimes it takes a painful situation to make us change our ways.
Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I obey your word.
It was good for me to be afflicted so that I might learn your decrees.
who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.''
Monday.action: Identify one personal weakness or 'thorn' you struggle with. Write it down and pray, asking God to show you His sufficient grace in that area today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that your grace is enough for me. In my weakness, help me to rely on your strength and not my own. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:2-3 (NIV) - 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a past difficulty. Write down one way that trial helped build your character or drew you closer to God.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, help me to see my trials through your eyes. Give me the faith to consider them joy, knowing you are at work. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 119:71 (NIV) - 'It was good for me to be afflicted so that I might learn your decrees.'
Wednesday.action: Read a chapter in Proverbs. Identify one piece of God's wisdom you may have overlooked until a painful experience taught you its truth.
Wednesday.prayer: God, thank you for teaching me through life's hard lessons. Open my heart to your instructions, which are more valuable than gold. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 (NIV) - '...who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.'
Thursday.action: Think of someone who is currently going through a trial similar to one you've experienced. Reach out to them with a message of encouragement or prayer.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide me to someone who needs the comfort you've given me. Use my story to bring them hope and point them to you. Amen.

Friday.scripture: Jeremiah 18:6 (NIV) - 'Like clay in the hand of the potter, so are you in my hand...'
Friday.action: Spend time in quiet prayer, surrendering an area where you feel 'broken' or 'marred' to God, the Potter. Ask Him to reshape you as He sees best.
Friday.prayer: Potter God, I am the clay. I surrender my broken pieces to you. Remake me, refine me, and let me reflect your image. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 10:9 (NIV) - 'If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.'
Saturday.action: If you have never made a decision to follow Christ, consider doing so today. If you have, write a simple prayer of thanksgiving for your salvation and share it with one person.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I believe you are Lord. I believe God raised you from the dead. I confess my sins and invite you into my life. Thank you for saving me. Amen.
